exemplar.title: What is the difference between the effects of a static or dynamic 10-minute warm-up routine on lower hamstring flexibility (measured in cm using the sit and reach test) among individuals aged 16-17?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

detailedExemplarData.title: What is the difference between the effects of a static or dynamic 10-minute warm-up routine on lower hamstring flexibility (measured in cm using the sit and reach test) among individuals aged 16-17?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

strengths: Links topic to personal ballet experience
Some procedural initiative shown through consent forms and control tables
weaknesses: Limited independent thinking beyond standard class-level procedures
Brief discussion of planning challenges and decision-making
Minimal personal reflection or novel input
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands were assessed at the lower band 1 level, indicating only limited personal engagement and initiative. While the connection to ballet and basic procedural input are evident, the investigation lacks the deeper reflection, clear personal significance, and substantial independent thinking required for a top-band score of 2. Thus a score of 1 is appropriate.

strengths: Research question is specific, relevant and clearly stated
Mostly appropriate and relevant background information with peer-reviewed citations
Systematic description of methodology including controls and ethics awareness
weaknesses: Background sections sometimes tangential and not always tied back to the research question
Methodology has key design limitations (between-subject design, unclear randomisation, limited reliability checks)
Ethical and safety considerations lack full detail on calibration and injury screening
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each of the four strands sits at the mid-band 3 (

strengths: Appropriate use of statistical tests (t-tests) and graphical representation with error bars
Calculation of key descriptive statistics (mean, median, SD, CV)
Recognition of variability and some discussion of potential sources of uncertainty
weaknesses: Incorrect interpretation of p-values and logical inconsistency in conclusions
Insufficient raw data completeness (missing values and small sample)
No numerical propagation of measurement uncertainty
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each of the four analysis strands was judged at band 3. The student demonstrates a sound grasp of data processing and basic interpretation but shows inconsistencies in accuracy and logical reasoning, and the consideration of uncertainty remains qualitative only. Thus a midpoint score of 3 is warranted.

strengths: Conclusion relates to hypothesis and cites data
Relevant comparisons drawn to physiological literature
Realistic and relevant suggestions for further study
weaknesses: Conclusion contains internal contradictions with non-significant results
Comparisons to scientific context are descriptive rather than critical
Limitations and suggestions lack detailed methodological specificity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All evaluation strands fall solidly in the mid band (3â4) but do not reach the depth or rigor for a 4. The student identifies limitations and proposes improvements but does not justify them in sufficient detail or critically assess the scientific context. A score of 3 accurately reflects this moderate performance.

strengths: Clear presentation aided by headings, tables and figures
Appropriate use of scientific and statistical terminology with minimal errors
weaknesses: Minor typographical errors and occasional awkward phrasing
Some figures lack full axis labels or unit clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication strands were consistently at the top band: the report is clear, well organized, focused, and uses subject-specific terminology correctly. Minor errors are present but do not impede understanding. Awarding the full 4 marks reflects this high level of communication quality.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student mentions instrument error, high SD, technique variation and participant differences; however, no numerical resolution, % uncertainty, or propagation is attempted. This equates to âsome considerationâ rather than âfull considerationâ.

Links are made to physiological mechanisms and literature, with several citations. Nonetheless, the comparison is descriptive and does not critically evaluate alignment or divergence with prior findings. Hence, it meets âsome relevant comparisonâ.
The report follows IB sections logically, includes variable tables and appendices, and information is rarely misplaced. Therefore, structure is âwell-structured with all necessary information.â

Suggestions include larger sample, gender analysis, long-term study and multiple observers. They are realistic and relevant but lack detailed methodological specifics (e.g., exact sampling strategy, statistical plan), so they sit comfortably within the 3â4 mark band.

The research question is specific (static vs dynamic warm-up, 10 min, sit-and-reach, 16-17 y). It is relevant and mostly focused, though it does not clarify whether improvements are acute or chronic and leaves ambiguity about group vs within-subject design. This aligns with the ârelevant but not fully focusedâ descriptor.

The evaluation discusses sample size, gender, test validity and human error, showing awareness of methodological issues, though depth (e.g., quantitative impact) is limited. This corresponds to the mid-band descriptor.

The method lists apparatus, procedure, controls and ethics. Nonetheless, key design flaws remain: different participants are used for each warm-up, randomisation is vague, sample size limited, and no reliability checks or counterbalancing are included. These shortcomings suggest a methodology that is âmainly appropriate with limitationsâ rather than fully appropriate.

The write-up supplies physiological background (sarcomere mechanics, proprioceptors) and cites several peer-reviewed sources. While broadly appropriate, sections are descriptive and sometimes tangential (e.g. DOMS, circulation) without tying directly to the RQ or expected outcomes. Depth and selectivity therefore correspond to the âmainly appropriate and relevantâ level.

Most content is relevant to addressing the research question, with limited digressions. Wordiness occasionally appears in background and evaluation, but overall focus is maintained, fulfilling the top band expectations.

Interpretation recognises that p > 0.05 indicates no significant difference, yet the narrative simultaneously claims the data âsupport the hypothesis,â showing confusion. Trends are discussed, but causal links are weak. Overall, validity is broadly acceptable but incomplete.
Headings, tables and figures aid comprehension; minor typographical errors and occasional awkward phrasing exist but do not hinder clarity. Presentation thus fits âclear with minimal errorsâ.

The student links the topic to personal ballet experience and selects participants of a similar age group; however, references to personal decision-making or challenges encountered during planning/implementation are brief. Independent thinking beyond choosing a common test (sit-and-reach) is limited. Hence, engagement is present but clearly modest rather than substantial.

Means, medians, SD, CV, and independent-samples t-tests are calculated and graphs include error bars. Nevertheless, some means in the text differ from the raw table, p-value interpretation is flawed, and paired rather than independent tests would have been more suitable. Processing is therefore âappropriate but inconsistentâ.

The introduction states that the student struggles to maintain flexibility and âhopes to provide insightâ; this conveys personal significance, yet the rationale remains general and lacks probing curiosity or reflection on alternative routes. Justification therefore meets the description of âlittle personal significanceâ rather than the stronger, well-developed rationale required for the top band.

Twenty raw scores per condition (plus before/after) are provided; this is adequate but not extensive, and there are three missing values. Data appear plausible but no photograph of full data sheet or replication trials is shown. Hence, relevance is clear yet completeness only partial.

Procedural steps, control tables and consent forms show some initiative, but they largely reproduce standard class-level practice. No evidence is provided of piloting, self-generated apparatus, or novel analytical techniques. Consequently, only limited personal input is evident.

Consent forms, withdrawal rights and nurse access display awareness of ethics; temperature control and supervision show safety consideration. However, calibration of equipment and screening for injury are only mentioned as uncertainties rather than planned safeguards. The strand therefore fits âsome awarenessâ rather than âfull awarenessâ.

Scientific terms (e.g., sarcomere, Golgi tendon organ) and statistical terminology are used correctly; citation style is fairly consistent. A few inaccuracies (e.g., âtet scoreâ) occur yet remain minimal, placing the work in the 3â4 mark band.

The conclusion restates main results and relates to the hypothesis, but internal contradictions (no significance yet âsupports hypothesisâ) limit precision. Evidence from data is cited, so relevance exists, but depth and rigour are moderate not detailed.

annotations.comment: The background section on muscle fibre function is thorough but veers into general physiology. Consider condensing and directly tying each concept to the studyâs research question.
The dynamic warm-up results section claims âsignificant changeâ despite t-test p = 0.652. Correct the interpretation to reflect non-significance.
A paired-samples t-test would be more appropriate for before-and-after measurements on the same participants. Re-analyse using the correct test to improve validity.
The table for the independent sample test is presented correctly, and the student recognizes non-significance. Good use of statistical tests to support the analysis.
The sit-and-reach limitations are discussed but end mid-sentence. Complete the critique of test validity and consider alternative flexibility assessments.
The extension section outlines valuable future studies on long-term effects and gender differences, showing realistic and relevant suggestions.
The opening sentence of the extension references a âsignificant correlationâ but no correlation analysis is presented. Clarify or remove.
The consent form is comprehensive, but ensure the appendix reference does not distract from the main report. Appendices are for supplementary details.
The research question is clear and relevant but could be refined by specifying whether the flexibility improvements are acute or chronic and clarifying the between- or within-subject design.

The introduction links the study to personal ballet experience, but the discussion of planning challenges and decision-making is brief. Adding reflective commentary on your investigative process would deepen personal engagement.

Several background paragraphs describe DOMS and circulation without directly linking these mechanisms to the effects of static vs dynamic warm-ups on flexibility. Focus on studies that predict expected outcomes.
Clarify why your modified sit-and-reach box uses a sliding design and how this may impact measurement accuracy and comparability with standard protocols.
The hypothesis is well-justified with physiological rationale and literature support, demonstrating good understanding of mechanisms.

The variables table lists appropriate controls, but using separate participant groups rather than a repeated measures design introduces potential between-subject variability and weakens causal inference.
The procedure is systematic, but details of the randomisation process (e.g. seed, blocking) are missing. Include explicit steps to strengthen methodological rigor.
Ethical considerations mention consent and supervision, but there is no plan for calibration checks on the sit-and-reach box. Outline calibration and pilot testing safeguards.

The raw data sample includes only four participants, which is insufficient to capture variability. A fuller dataset or replication trials should be presented in the main text rather than only in the appendix.
Processed data tables are accurate, but there is no propagation of measurement uncertainty. Include calculations of instrument resolution or percentage uncertainties.
The narrative summary restates table values but offers limited insight into data distribution or potential outliers. Consider deeper statistical commentary on variance and skew.
The static warm-up figure lacks clear axis labels and units, which hinders interpretation. Label axes fully and ensure font sizes are legible.
The interpretation acknowledges p > 0.05 yet asserts support for the hypothesis, creating a logical inconsistency. Align your conclusions with statistical outcomes.
Several sentences (e.g., âreaching the sensesâ) are unclear and contain typos. Edit for grammatical accuracy and clarity of description.
The narrative cites M = 14.03 for post-static warm-up, but the table lists 13.49. Check all numerical references for consistency.
Measurement uncertainty is discussed qualitatively, but numeric estimates of instrument error or participant positioning variability would strengthen the section.
You note that randomisation was not fully specifiedâconsider describing block or stratified randomisation methods to control for baseline flexibility differences.
The conclusion restates findings but contradicts the non-significant results by claiming dynamic warm-ups are superior. Revise to align with statistical evidence.
Include personal insights on challenges faced during pilot testing or data collection to demonstrate deeper personal engagement and reflection.
The evaluation correctly identifies strengths such as objective measurement and controlled variables, demonstrating good methodological awareness.
Several references list URLs without consistent formatting or retrieval dates. Apply uniform IB citation conventions throughout.
Reference entries vary in style (e.g., italics, volume numbers). Standardise formatting for journal titles and article details to improve consistency.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: Justification shows little personal significance or curiosity
Little evidence of personal input in design, implementation or presentation
Limited evidence of personal engagement with little independent thinking
Clear evidence of personal input and initiative throughout the investigation
Clear evidence of personal engagement with significant independent thinking
Justification demonstrates clear personal significance and curiosity

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Evidence of personal engagement and independent thinking

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: Superficial or limited relevance background information
Research question stated but not focused
Methodology appropriate to very limited extent
Mainly appropriate and relevant background information
Relevant but not fully focused research question
Entirely appropriate and relevant background information
Highly appropriate methodology addressing all significant factors
Relevant and fully focused research question
Sufficient relevant raw data supporting detailed conclusion
Basic outline of practical issues only
Some relevant comparison to scientific context
Detailed, relevant and fully supported conclusion
Clear understanding of methodological issues demonstrated
Correct and justified comparison to scientific context
Many errors in terminology and conventions
Appropriate use of terminology with minimal errors
Well structured with all necessary information
